Tuscan Salmon: Pat salmon fillets dry and season both sides with salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear salmon skin-side up for 4 minutes, then flip and cook for a further 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sun-dried tomatoes and stir for 1 minute.
Pour in the heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer, stirring to combine.
Add fresh spinach and stir until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Return the salmon to the pan and spoon the sauce over the fillets. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Serve immediately topped with grated Parmesan cheese.
Carbonara: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook spaghetti until al dente according to package instructions. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
While the pasta cooks, whisk together eggs, Pecorino Romano, and Parmesan in a bowl. Season generously with black pepper.
Cook pancetta or guanciale in a large pan over medium heat until crispy. Add garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute, then remove garlic.
Remove the pan from heat. Add the drained pasta and toss to coat in the rendered fat.
Working quickly, pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing constantly. Add pasta water a little at a time to loosen the sauce into a creamy consistency.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and a generous grind of black pepper.
Chicken Tacos: Mix cumin, smoked paprika, chilli powder, and a pinch of salt together in a bowl. Toss the sliced chicken breast in the spice mix until fully coated.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken for 5–6 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through and slightly charred. Squeeze over the lime juice.
Warm the flour tortillas in a dry pan for 20–30 seconds each side, or wrap in a damp paper towel and microwave for 30 seconds.
Lay out the warm tortillas and divide the chicken evenly between them.
Top with fresh salsa, sliced avocado, and a dollop of sour cream.
Finish with fresh coriander and an extra squeeze of lime. Serve immediately.
American Grilled Chicken Wrap: Season chicken breasts with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Brush with olive oil.
Grill chicken over medium-high heat for 6–7 minutes per side until cooked through. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Warm the flour tortillas in a dry pan or microwave for 20 seconds each.
Lay out each tortilla and drizzle with ranch dressing.
Top with shredded romaine, diced tomato, red onion, cheddar cheese, and sliced chicken.
Fold in the sides, then roll up tightly. Slice in half and serve immediately.
Argentinian Grilled Steak Salad: Take the flank steak out of the fridge 20 minutes before cooking. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat a griddle or frying pan over high heat. Brush the steak with 1 tbsp olive oil and cook for 3–4 minutes each side for medium-rare. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
While the steak rests, toss the salad greens, sliced red onion, red bell pepper, and cherry tomatoes together in a large bowl.
Whisk together the remaining olive oil, chimichurri sauce, and red wine vinegar to make the dressing.
Arrange the salad on plates and top with the sliced steak.
Drizzle the dressing over the top and serve immediately.
Australian Avocado Toast with Egg: Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
Halve the avocado and remove the stone. Scoop the flesh into a bowl, add lemon juice, salt, and black pepper, and mash to your preferred consistency.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Fry the eggs to your liking — sunny side up works best.
Spread the mashed avocado generously over each slice of toast.
Place a fried egg on top of each toast.
Finish with crumbled feta cheese and a pinch of chilli flakes. Serve immediately.
Brazilian Black Bean Bowl: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic, diced red bell pepper, cumin, and paprika. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the cooked black beans and stir to combine. Cook for 5 minutes until heated through.
Squeeze over the lime juice and season with salt to taste.
Divide the cooked brown rice between bowls and spoon the black bean mixture on top.
Garnish with fresh coriander and an extra wedge of lime.
British Baked Salmon with Veg: Place the halved baby potatoes on a large baking tray. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, season with salt and pepper, and roast for 15 minutes.
Add the broccoli florets, sliced carrot, and courgette to the tray. Drizzle with the remaining olive oil and toss to coat.
Place the salmon fillets on the tray among the vegetables. Drizzle with lemon juice and sprinkle with dried dill, salt, and pepper.
Return to the oven and bake for a further 15 minutes, until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.
Chinese Stir Fry Vegetables: Mix the cornflour with 2 tbsp cold water in a small bowl and set aside.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat until smoking. Add sesame oil.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the carrot and broccoli first and stir-fry for 2 minutes. Then add the bell pepper, snap peas, and mushrooms.
Continue stir-frying for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables are tender but still have a bite.
Add the soy sauce and cornflour mixture. Toss everything together and cook for 1 minute until the sauce thickens.
Serve immediately over steamed rice or noodles.
Egyptian Ful Medames: Warm the cooked fava beans in a saucepan over medium heat with a splash of water for 5 minutes.
Drain most of the liquid, then lightly mash the beans with a fork, leaving some whole for texture.
Add the minced garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, cumin, and diced tomato. Stir well to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper. Cook for a further 2–3 minutes until everything is heated through.
Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped parsley and a drizzle of extra olive oil.
Ethiopian Lentil Stew: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 8 minutes until golden.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the berbere spice and tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Rinse the red lentils and add them to the pot along with the vegetable stock.
Bring to the boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are soft and the stew has thickened.
Season with salt to taste and serve with injera or flatbread.
Filipino Chicken Tinola: Heat a large pot over medium heat. Add a drizzle of oil and sauté the sliced onion for 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and ginger slices and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken thighs and cook for 4–5 minutes, turning until lightly browned on all sides.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to the boil. Skim off any foam that rises to the surface.
Add the fish sauce, reduce the heat, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the sliced green papaya and cook for 5 minutes until just tender.
Stir in the spinach or malunggay leaves and cook for 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper.
French Ratatouille: Heat olive oil in a large, deep pan or casserole dish over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the aubergine and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the courgette, red bell pepper, and yellow bell pepper. Cook for a further 5 minutes.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and dried thyme. Season with salt and black pepper.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25–30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all vegetables are tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve warm as a side or main dish.
German Chicken Vegetable Soup: Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and minced garlic and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the diced chicken breast and cook for 4–5 minutes until sealed on all sides.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to the boil.
Add the sliced carrots, chopped celery, diced potatoes, and sliced leek. Season with salt and pepper.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 25 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the chopped parsley and adjust seasoning.
Ladle into bowls and serve with crusty bread.
Greek Chicken Salad: Season the chicken breasts with salt, pepper, and a pinch of dried oregano. Brush with olive oil.
Grill or pan-fry the chicken over medium-high heat for 6–7 minutes each side until cooked through. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
In a large bowl, combine the romaine lettuce, chopped cucumber, cherry tomatoes, sliced red onion, olives, and feta cheese.
Whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, and dried oregano to make the dressing. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Top with the sliced grilled chicken and serve immediately.
Hawaiian Poke Bowl: Cook the sushi rice according to package instructions and allow to cool slightly.
In a bowl, gently toss the diced tuna with soy sauce and sesame oil. Leave to marinate for 5 minutes.
Divide the sushi rice between two bowls.
Arrange the marinated tuna, sliced avocado, diced cucumber, and shredded carrot over the rice.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and sliced spring onions.
Top with nori strips and serve immediately with extra soy sauce on the side.
Indian Lentil Dal: Heat ghee or oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 8 minutes until golden.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the turmeric, cumin, and garam masala and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes until they break down into a sauce.
Rinse the red lentils and add them to the pan with 3 cups of water. Bring to the boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are completely soft.
Season with salt and serve topped with fresh coriander alongside rice or flatbread.
Indonesian Gado Gado Salad: Blanch the mixed vegetables in boiling salted water for 2–3 minutes until just tender. Drain and refresh under cold water.
Pan-fry or bake the cubed tofu until golden and crisp on all sides.
Boil the eggs for 8 minutes, then peel and halve them.
Make the peanut dressing by whisking together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, chilli paste, and 2–3 tbsp warm water until smooth.
Arrange the blanched vegetables, bean sprouts, sliced cucumber, tofu, and boiled eggs on a serving platter.
Drizzle generously with the peanut dressing and finish with crispy shallots.
Italian Caprese Salad: Slice the tomatoes and fresh mozzarella into even rounds, approximately 1cm thick.
Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a serving plate, overlapping slightly.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices.
Drizzle extra virgin olive oil evenly over the top.
Drizzle the balsamic glaze over the salad.
Season with salt and black pepper and serve immediately.
Japanese Teriyaki Salmon: Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, honey, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, and sesame oil in a bowl to make the teriyaki sauce.
Place the salmon fillets in a shallow dish and pour half the sauce over them. Marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat. Remove the salmon from the marinade and cook skin-side down for 4 minutes.
Flip the salmon, pour over the remaining sauce, and cook for a further 3–4 minutes, basting with the sauce as it reduces.
Cook until the sauce is sticky and the salmon is cooked through.
Serve over steamed rice and garnish with sliced spring onions and sesame seeds.
Jordanian Chicken Freekeh Bowl: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic, diced carrot, allspice, and cinnamon. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken and cook for 5 minutes, stirring, until sealed on all sides.
Rinse the freekeh and add it to the pot along with the chicken stock. Bring to the boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes until the freekeh is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff with a fork, season with salt and pepper, and serve topped with toasted almonds.
Korean Bibimbap: Cook the rice and keep warm. Season the beef strips with 1 tbsp soy sauce and a drizzle of sesame oil. Stir-fry in a hot pan for 3–4 minutes until cooked. Set aside.
Blanch the spinach in boiling water for 30 seconds, drain, and squeeze dry. Season with a little sesame oil and salt.
Stir-fry the julienned carrot and courgette separately in a little oil for 2 minutes each. Season lightly.
Blanch the bean sprouts for 1 minute, drain, and season with sesame oil.
Fry the eggs sunny side up in a lightly oiled pan.
Divide the rice between bowls. Arrange the beef, vegetables, and bean sprouts in sections on top.
Place a fried egg in the centre. Add a dollop of gochujang and drizzle with sesame oil.
Lebanese Fattoush Salad: Preheat your oven to 200°C. Tear or cut the pita bread into bite-sized pieces, drizzle with a little olive oil, and bake for 8–10 minutes until golden and crisp.
Combine the mixed lettuce, chopped tomatoes, cucumber, sliced red onion, radishes, parsley, and mint in a large bowl.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well.
Add the toasted pita pieces on top just before serving so they stay crisp.
Serve immediately with an extra sprinkle of sumac.
Mediterranean Chickpea Bowl: Cook the quinoa according to package instructions and allow to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the chickpeas, diced cucumber, cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, olives, and feta cheese.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, dried oregano, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Divide the quinoa between bowls and top with the chickpea mixture.
Drizzle the dressing over the top and serve.
Mexican Chicken Burrito Bowl: Season the sliced chicken with cumin, chilli powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken for 5–6 minutes each side until cooked through and slightly charred. Slice or shred.
Warm the black beans in a small saucepan with a pinch of cumin and salt.
Divide the cooked rice between bowls.
Top with the chicken, black beans, corn, and fresh salsa.
Add sliced avocado, shredded cheddar, and a dollop of sour cream.
Moroccan Chickpea Tagine: Heat olive oil in a tagine or heavy-based pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and cinnamon. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the sliced carrot and diced courgette and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes, drained chickpeas, and raisins. Stir well to combine.
Add a splash of water or vegetable stock, cover, and simmer over low heat for 25 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with couscous or flatbread.
Nepalese Vegetable Curry: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the turmeric and cumin and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes until they break down.
Add the diced potato and sliced carrot. Stir to coat in the spices and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the cauliflower florets and peas. Pour in enough water to just cover the vegetables.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and serve with steamed rice or flatbread.
Peruvian Quinoa Bowl: Cook the quinoa according to package instructions. Fluff with a fork and season lightly with salt.
Roast the cubed sweet potato: toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper, and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden.
Warm the black beans in a pan with a pinch of salt.
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Divide the quinoa between bowls. Arrange the roasted sweet potato, black beans, corn, sliced avocado, and diced red onion on top.
Drizzle with the lime dressing and garnish with fresh coriander.
Spanish Grilled Shrimp with Rice: Cook the rice in salted water with the saffron strands for the recommended time. Drain and keep warm.
Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and diced red bell pepper. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add the chopped tomato and smoked paprika. Cook for 3–4 minutes until the tomato breaks down.
Increase the heat to high. Add the peeled shrimp and cook for 2 minutes each side until pink and cooked through.
Squeeze lemon juice over the shrimp and season with salt.
Serve the shrimp over the saffron rice and garnish with fresh parsley.
Swedish Salmon Salad: Cook the baby potatoes in boiling salted water for 15 minutes until tender. Drain and allow to cool.
If the salmon isn't already cooked, pan-fry or bake until cooked through, then flake into large pieces.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, Greek yogurt, and chopped dill. Season with salt and black pepper.
Arrange the salad greens on a serving plate. Top with the sliced cucumber, red onion, and baby potatoes.
Flake the salmon over the top.
Drizzle with the dill dressing and serve immediately.
Thai Basil Chicken: Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Add the sliced red chillies and minced garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the sliced onion and red bell pepper and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken mince and break it up with a spoon. Stir-fry for 5–6 minutes until cooked through.
Add the soy sauce, oyster sauce, and fish sauce. Toss everything together.
Remove from the heat and stir in the Thai basil leaves until just wilted.
Turkish Chicken Shish Kebab: In a bowl, combine grated onion, minced garlic, olive oil, yogurt, paprika, cumin, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the cubed chicken to the marinade, toss well to coat, and leave to marinate for at least 1 hour (or overnight in the fridge).
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Grill over medium-high heat for 12–15 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and lightly charred.
Rest for 2 minutes, then serve on flatbread with a side salad and yogurt dipping sauce.
Vietnamese Fresh Spring Rolls: Prepare all fillings: cook the rice vermicelli according to package instructions, drain and rinse under cold water. Peel and halve the cooked shrimp lengthwise.
Set up a station with a wide shallow dish of warm water, and all fillings laid out.
Dip one rice paper wrapper in the warm water for 10–15 seconds until just pliable. Lay it flat on a clean surface.
In the lower third of the wrapper, layer a few pieces of lettuce, vermicelli, cucumber, carrot, mint, and coriander.
Place 3–4 shrimp halves across the middle of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom of the wrapper up over the filling, then fold in the sides, and roll up tightly like a burrito.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Serve with hoisin dipping sauce and a sprinkle of crushed peanuts.
Protein Pancakes: In a large bowl, whisk together Greek yogurt, eggs, vanilla extract, and honey until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and stir until just combined. Do not overmix; some lumps are okay.
Heat a griddle or non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly coat with cooking spray.
Pour 1/4 cup of batter for each pancake onto the griddle. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges look set, about 2-3 minutes.
Flip and cook for another 2 minutes until golden brown.
Serve warm with your favorite toppings like maple syrup, fresh berries, or extra Greek yogurt.
Protein French Toast: In a shallow bowl, whisk together eggs, cottage cheese, cinnamon, sweetener, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly coat with cooking spray.
Dip each bread slice into the egg mixture, coating both sides well.
Place the coated bread slices in the hot pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Serve immediately topped with Greek yogurt, fresh berries, sliced fruit, or a drizzle of maple syrup.
Greek Yogurt Parfait: In a glass or bowl, add half of the Greek yogurt as the first layer.
Add half of the berries and granola on top of the yogurt.
Add the remaining Greek yogurt as the second layer.
Top with the remaining berries, granola, and sliced nuts.
Drizzle honey over the top and sprinkle with chia seeds if using.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
Veggie Scrambled Eggs: In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Heat olive oil or butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes until softened.
Add cherry tomatoes and spinach, cooking until the spinach wilts, about 1 minute.
Pour the egg mixture into the pan. Let it sit for a few seconds, then gently stir with a spatula, folding the eggs as they cook.
When the eggs are almost set but still slightly creamy, remove from heat. Stir in cheese if using.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately with toast if desired.
Peanut Butter Banana Smoothie Bowl: Add the frozen banana, peanut butter, milk, protein powder (if using), and cinnamon to a blender.
Blend on high until smooth and thick. If too thick, add a splash more milk. If too thin, add ice or more frozen banana.
Pour the smoothie into a bowl.
Top with granola, sliced fresh banana, chia seeds, and a drizzle of honey.
Microwave Mug Omelette: Lightly spray a microwave-safe mug with cooking spray.
Crack the eggs into the mug and add milk, salt, and pepper. Whisk with a fork until well combined.
Stir in the diced bell pepper, tomato, onion, and cheese.
Microwave on high for 1 minute. Stir gently with a fork.
Microwave for another 1 to 1.5 minutes, or until the eggs are fully set and cooked through.
Let cool for 30 seconds, then enjoy directly from the mug or slide onto a plate.
Chickpea Salad Sandwich: In a medium bowl, mash the chickpeas with a fork or potato masher until mostly broken down, leaving some chunks for texture.
Add mayo, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, celery, red onion, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Divide the chickpea salad between two slices of bread and top with lettuce and tomato.
Close the sandwiches with the remaining bread slices. Slice in half and serve immediately.
Tuna Pasta Salad: In a large bowl, combine the cooked and cooled pasta, drained tuna, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, and red onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the pasta mixture and toss until everything is well coated.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Garnish with fresh parsley or basil.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Hummus Veggie Wrap: Lay the tortilla flat on a clean surface.
Spread the hummus evenly over the center of the tortilla, leaving a 1-inch border.
Layer the salad greens, cucumber, bell pepper, carrot, and red cabbage on top of the hummus.
Sprinkle with sunflower seeds and season with salt and pepper.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla, then roll it up tightly from the bottom.
Slice in half and serve immediately, or wrap in foil for a portable meal.
Caprese Sandwich: Drizzle the bread slices with olive oil if desired.
Layer mozzarella slices on two slices of bread.
Top with tomato slices and fresh basil leaves.
Drizzle balsamic glaze over the top.
Close the sandwiches with the remaining bread slices. Slice in half and serve immediately.
Cottage Cheese Bowl: Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with fresh berries or sliced fruit.
Sprinkle granola, chopped nuts, and chia seeds on top.
Drizzle with honey and add a pinch of cinnamon.
Quick Veggie Stir Fry with Noodles: Cook the noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat.
Add bell pepper, broccoli, carrot, and snap peas. Stir fry for 4-5 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the cooked noodles to the pan along with soy sauce, sesame oil, and honey. Toss everything together for 2 minutes.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Serve immediately.
Quick Fried Rice: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble until cooked. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the pan. Add onion and garlic, cooking for 1 minute.
Add frozen mixed vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes until thawed and tender.
Add the cooked rice, breaking up any clumps. Stir fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return the scrambled eggs to the pan. Add soy sauce and sesame oil, tossing to combine.
Garnish with sliced green onions. Serve hot.
Loaded Baked Sweet Potato: Pierce the sweet potatoes several times with a fork.
Place them on a microwave-safe plate and microwave on high for 5-8 minutes, turning halfway through, until tender.
While the potatoes cook, warm the black beans in a small pot or microwave.
Carefully slice the sweet potatoes open lengthwise and fluff the insides with a fork.
Top each potato with black beans, shredded cheese, avocado slices, salsa, and a dollop of Greek yogurt.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve immediately.
Turkey & Cheese Roll-Ups: Lay out the turkey slices on a clean surface.
Spread a thin layer of mustard or mayo on each turkey slice.
Place a slice of cheese on top of each turkey slice, folding if needed.
Add a lettuce leaf, cucumber stick, and bell pepper stick on one end.
Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roll up tightly from one end to the other.
Secure with toothpicks if needed. Serve immediately or refrigerate for later.
Protein Pudding: In a bowl, add the Greek yogurt and protein powder.
Mix together until smooth and well combined.
If using, add cocoa powder and mix until fully incorporated.
Top with fresh fruit if desired.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for 30 minutes for a thicker texture.
Banana Protein Cookies: Preheat oven to 340°F (170°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, use a fork or potato masher to mash the ripe bananas until smooth.
Add oats and protein powder, then stir the mixture into a dough. If the dough is too wet, add more oats. If it's too dry, mix in a splash of milk.
Scoop the protein dough onto the lined baking tray, dividing it into 8 to 10 portions. Shape each into a ball, then gently flatten into cookies.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly golden on top. Don't overbake or they'll get dry.
Let cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
Protein Cheesecake: Preheat oven to 320°F (160°C) and line the base and sides of a 6-inch springform cake mold with parchment paper.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together eggs, granular sweetener, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Add Greek yogurt and cream cheese to the mixture, stirring thoroughly until all is well combined.
Add cornstarch gradually, mixing to prevent lumps.
Transfer the yogurt mixture to your prepared cake mold and bake for 40 to 50 minutes.
Let cool completely to room temperature in the mold.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 8 hours before slicing.
Top with fresh strawberries and sugar-free jam if desired. Serve chilled.
Protein Smoothie: Add banana, milk, protein powder, and peanut butter to a blender.
Add ice cubes if desired for a thicker consistency.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.
Peanut Butter Banana Toast: Toast the bread slices until golden and crispy.
Spread peanut butter evenly over each slice of toast.
Overnight Chilled Chia: To each of four 16-ounce jars, add 1/2 cup oats, 1 tablespoon chia seeds, 1 tablespoon honey, and 2/3 cup milk.
Refrigerate overnight or for at least 4 hours.
In the morning, stir and enjoy cold or at room temperature.
Breakfast Burrito: Preheat oven broiler with rack 6 inches from heat.
Arrange tomatillos, jalapeño (cut sides down), and onion wedges on a foil-lined baking sheet. Broil until blistered, 10-12 minutes.
Let vegetables cool, then transfer to food processor. Add lime juice, cilantro, and 1/4 tsp salt. Pulse to combine (salsa should be slightly chunky).
Beat eggs with 1 tbsp water and 1/4 tsp salt. Heat oil in large nonstick skillet on medium. Add eggs and cook, stirring every few seconds, to desired doneness, 2-3 minutes. Fold in 1/2 cup cheese.
Spread 1/4 cup beans on each tortilla, then divide eggs and remaining cheese on top.
Spoon 2 tablespoons salsa over each and roll, folding sides over filling and then rolling from the bottom up.
If desired, crisp both sides in nonstick skillet on medium. Serve with remaining salsa.
Sweet Potato Kale Frittata: Preheat oven to 350°F. Whisk together eggs, half-and-half, salt, and pepper.
Sauté sweet potatoes in 1 tablespoon hot oil in a 10-inch ovenproof nonstick skillet over medium heat 8-10 minutes or until tender and golden; remove and keep warm.
Sauté kale, red onion, and garlic in remaining 1 tablespoon oil 3-4 minutes or until kale is wilted and tender; stir in potatoes.
Pour egg mixture evenly over vegetables, and cook 3 more minutes. Sprinkle egg mixture with goat cheese.
Bake at 350°F for 10-14 minutes or until set.
Let cool for a few minutes, then slice and serve warm.
